prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline (C₆H₅NH₂) was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, density-fitted, PySCF backend) in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral ground state). The calculation also computed open-shell N+1 (cation, doublet) and N−1 (anion, doublet) states to obtain Fukui indices. The input geometry was a fixed structure from the provided .xyz file; this was not re-optimized at the DFT level. Mulliken partial charges and Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) were computed for all 14 atoms. The most electrophilic site is carbon at position 3 (ortho to the amino group, f⁺ = 2.298), and the most nucleophilic site is the nitrogen atom (f⁻ = 0.205). Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, confirming both the N+1 and N−1 states converged properly. The SCF converged and the engine's integrity status is trustworthy. A Fukui reactivity visualization plot was generated. This is a structure-building/property task (not a single numeric value), so n_atoms is reported instead of a value.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
